pi_items.source: Code analysis of seed_bahmni.py lines 265-269: James Osei is enumerated as i=11 (11th patient), yielding identifier f'BAH{11+1:06d}' = BAH000012. BAH000011 corresponds to Emily Chen (10th patient).
setup_task.sh lines 14-34 creates Person Attribute Type named 'Occupation'; verifier.py line 6 and 48 reference 'Occupation' as the attribute name.
setup_task.sh line 79-80: Sets initial occupation attribute value to 'Unemployed' via API call; line 91 stores 'Unemployed' in task context.
verifier.py line 48: Defines target_value as 'School Teacher'; used in line 52 for case-insensitive matching verification.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves updating patient James Osei's Occupation attribute in a synthetic Bahmni/OpenMRS environment. Critical discrepancy: the metadata claims patient identifier BAH000011, but code analysis confirms James Osei is assigned BAH000012 by the seeding script (seed_bahmni.py), while BAH000011 belongs to patient Emily Chen. The attribute is a custom-created Person Attribute Type. The initial value is set to 'Unemployed' and the target update value is 'School Teacher'. All other metadata claims (name, attribute name, initial/target values) are verified against source code.
